_note: Standalone explainer data for how soccer is organized across club and international football.

tiers.title: Club football is the day job
tiers.label: Club
tiers.summary: Most players spend most of the year with their clubs, not their national teams.
tiers.whyItMatters: This is where players build weekly chemistry, learn a manager's system, and play the majority of their matches.
tiers.watcherTranslation: If you know American sports, club football is the regular-season home of the stars you recognize.

tiers.title: Domestic leagues decide who is best over time
tiers.label: League
tiers.summary: Leagues reward consistency because teams play long schedules instead of one-off elimination games.
tiers.whyItMatters: A great league team can still struggle in a short tournament because tournament football punishes mistakes differently.
tiers.watcherTranslation: Leagues are the marathon version of the sport.

tiers.title: Domestic cups are where chaos lives
tiers.label: Cup
tiers.summary: Cup competitions are usually knockout-heavy, which makes giant-killings and weird nights much more common.
tiers.whyItMatters: They teach fans the emotional rhythm of win-or-go-home football before they even get to the World Cup.
tiers.watcherTranslation: If leagues are the marathon, cups are the upset machine.

tiers.title: Continental club tournaments raise the level
tiers.label: Continental Club
tiers.summary: The best clubs from each country also face one another in regional competitions.
tiers.whyItMatters: This is often where casual fans first see star players outside domestic league play.
tiers.watcherTranslation: This is where elite clubs stop being local kings and have to prove it abroad.

tiers.title: International football is the short-camp version
tiers.label: International
tiers.summary: National teams only get players in shorter windows, which makes identity and fit just as important as raw talent.
tiers.whyItMatters: A national team cannot buy fixes or train together every day, so tournament teams often look more pragmatic than club superteams.
tiers.watcherTranslation: The talent may be huge, but the prep time is tiny.

tiers.title: Regional tournaments are dress rehearsals for the biggest stage
tiers.label: Regional Tournaments
tiers.summary: Every confederation has its own major national-team event with its own style, rivalries, and pressure.
tiers.whyItMatters: These tournaments show who is battle-tested before the World Cup arrives.
tiers.watcherTranslation: Think of them as continent-level measuring sticks.

tiers.title: The World Cup is the event that bends everything else around it
tiers.label: World Cup
tiers.summary: It is the most global and emotionally concentrated tournament in the sport.
tiers.whyItMatters: Players can have legendary club careers and still be judged by what happened at a World Cup.
tiers.watcherTranslation: This is the tournament even non-fans feel.
